sources.notes: Choose rewards around a variable x and optimize the worst-case outcome.
Official tutorial separates x-a_i and a_i-x choices and handles p<=q / q<=p cases.
Maspy notes that choosing both sides for an index is equivalent to doing nothing, leading to independent sorted prefix choices.

annotation.summary: Maximize a worst-case score involving choices of |x-a_i| contributions.
annotation.constraints: n is large enough that after sorting, only prefix/suffix aggregate calculations are intended.
annotation.core_idea: Although absolute values appear, each i independently contributes either x-a_i, a_i-x, both, or neither, and choosing both is equivalent to not changing the optimum. If p terms of the first type and q terms of the second type are chosen, the worst x is one endpoint depending on whether p<=q or q<=p. Therefore sort a, precompute prefix/suffix sums, and maximize f(p)+g(q) under the two cases.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) per test case.
tricks: split absolute value into two independent choices
endpoint worst case based on p versus q

solution_variants.name: sorted-prefix-suffix-optimization
solution_variants.summary: Sort values, precompute prefix/suffix sums, and optimize the two cases p<=q and q<=p.

tags.evidence: The optimal selected values for each side are prefixes/suffixes after sorting.
Scores f(p) and g(q) are evaluated from prefix/suffix aggregate sums.
For fixed counts, the chosen a_i are greedily the smallest/largest values after sorting.
